tasks.title: Replace Visual Assets Browser with dropdown selectors globally
tasks.description: The Visual Assets Browser (AssetBrowserDialog.tsx) should be removed and replaced with simple dropdown selectors everywhere it is used. (1) Audit all usages of AssetBrowserDialog across the admin panel: BuildingConfigurationPanel.tsx, ConfigDetailPanel.tsx, GroundConfigPanel.tsx, CharacterConfigPanel.tsx, NatureConfigPanel.tsx, ItemConfigPanel.tsx, and any other files. (2) Replace each AssetBrowserDialog invocation with a dropdown (<select> or Radix Select component) that lists available assets from the asset collection. (3) Each dropdown should show the asset name (not just the ID) and ideally a small thumbnail or icon next to the name. (4) Group dropdown options by asset type or category where relevant (e.g., textures grouped by material type). (5) Remove the AssetBrowserDialog component and its imports once all usages are replaced. (6) Also remove the 'all-assets' browse mode from AdminAssetsHub if it relied on the browser dialog.

tasks.title: Fix texture asset dropdown to show asset names instead of IDs
tasks.description: The Texture Asset dropdown in GroundConfigPanel and other panels only shows an asset ID, making it impossible to tell what asset is selected. (1) In GroundConfigPanel.tsx and all other panels that show texture/asset dropdowns, resolve asset IDs to their human-readable names using the asset collection metadata. (2) If an asset has a filename like 'cobblestone_path.png', show 'Cobblestone Path' as the display name. (3) Parse the asset path to extract a readable name: strip extension, replace underscores/hyphens with spaces, capitalize words. (4) Show the resolved name as the primary label with the ID in smaller text or as a tooltip. (5) Apply this fix everywhere asset IDs are displayed to users.

tasks.title: Fix ground/terrain texture preview to show realistic surface appearance
tasks.description: The ground/terrain, road, and sidewalk texture preview currently shows just a colored circle, which gives no indication of how the texture will look. (1) In GroundConfigPanel.tsx, replace the colored circle preview with a rectangular surface preview that tiles the selected texture across a flat plane. (2) For ground textures, render a square patch showing the texture tiled as it would appear on terrain. (3) For road textures, render a road-segment-shaped preview (rectangular strip with optional lane markings). (4) For sidewalk textures, render a narrow rectangular strip preview. (5) When in 'procedural' mode with just a color, show the flat color on the appropriately shaped preview. (6) When in 'asset' mode with a texture selected, load and display the actual texture image tiled on the preview surface. (7) Add tiling controls that update the preview in real-time so users can see the effect of tiling changes.

tasks.title: Fix tree, vegetation, water feature, and rock editing previews
tasks.description: The editing preview is broken for trees, vegetation, water features, and rocks in the admin panel. (1) In NatureConfigPanel.tsx and related admin panel components, identify why the 3D model preview fails to render for nature assets. (2) Common issues: incorrect asset path resolution, missing SceneLoader configuration, preview canvas not initializing properly, or asset URLs not being constructed correctly from asset collection paths. (3) Fix the preview rendering pipeline: ensure the preview creates a Babylon.js scene, loads the selected .glb/.gltf model, positions the camera to frame the model, and adds appropriate lighting. (4) For each nature category (trees, vegetation, water_features, rocks), ensure the preview loads and displays the selected model. (5) If a model fails to load, show a clear error message with the attempted path instead of a blank preview. (6) Add a fallback placeholder mesh (colored shape appropriate to the category) when no model is selected.

tasks.title: Fix in-world object, prop, and quest item editing previews
tasks.description: The editing preview is broken for in-world objects, props, and quest items. (1) In ItemConfigPanel.tsx and any related panels, identify the preview rendering code for object/prop/item assets. (2) Fix the same class of issues as nature previews: asset path resolution, SceneLoader initialization, preview canvas setup. (3) Ensure the preview loads the selected .glb/.gltf model and displays it with proper lighting and camera framing. (4) For items that use procedural geometry (boxes, cylinders for basic items), ensure the procedural preview generates the correct shape with appropriate colors. (5) Test with various item types: weapons, tools, food items, furniture, containers. (6) Add auto-framing so the camera adjusts based on the model's bounding box size.

tasks.title: Fix interior texture and furniture preview in admin panel
tasks.description: Interior texture settings and furniture selections do not reflect in the admin panel preview. (1) In ConfigDetailPanel.tsx (the right-panel detail editor with 3D preview), fix the interior preview to actually render: wall textures on walls, floor textures on the floor plane, ceiling textures on the ceiling. (2) When a furniture set is selected, the preview should show representative furniture pieces placed in the preview room. (3) When a lighting preset is changed, the preview lighting should update accordingly. (4) Ensure the preview uses the same material/texture loading code as the in-game BuildingInteriorGenerator so what you see in the preview matches the game. (5) Add a small indicator showing the current furniture set name and lighting preset in the preview corner.

tasks.title: Standardize all admin panel asset selection to use consistent dropdown pattern
tasks.description: After removing the Visual Assets Browser, ensure all asset selection across the admin panel follows a consistent UI pattern. (1) Create a reusable AssetDropdown component that: accepts an asset type filter (model, texture, audio), loads available assets from the collection, displays them in a searchable dropdown with name and optional thumbnail, supports clearing the selection, shows a small inline preview of the selected asset. (2) Replace all ad-hoc asset selection UI with this component in: BuildingConfigurationPanel (exterior/interior textures, model selection), GroundConfigPanel (ground/road/sidewalk textures), CharacterConfigPanel (body models, hair models), NatureConfigPanel (tree/vegetation/rock/water models), ItemConfigPanel (item models). (3) Ensure the component handles loading states, empty collections, and missing assets gracefully.
